market_session: US equity market holiday (no regular session)
text: US equity markets were closed on Friday, June 19, 2026 for Juneteenth National Independence Day. The NYSE holiday calendar lists Juneteenth on Friday, June 19, and the Nasdaq US holiday schedule lists June 19, 2026 as Closed, so there was no regular US cash equity session on the report date.

The US bond market was also closed on June 19, 2026. SIFMA's 2026 holiday schedule lists Juneteenth (Friday, June 19) as a full close for trading in US dollar-denominated fixed-income securities, with no early-close time attached.

market_session: next US regular session after the Juneteenth holiday
text: US equity markets reopen for regular trading on Monday, June 22, 2026, the next session after the Juneteenth closure.

market_session: US regular session close on 2026-06-18 (last session before the Juneteenth holiday)

calculation.method: reported close and daily move from AP closing-index ledger

text: Going into the holiday, the last US regular session (Thursday, June 18, 2026) closed higher: the S&P 500 rose 80.48 points (1.1%) to 7,500.58; the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 72.15 points (0.1%) to 51,564.70; the Nasdaq Composite rose 496.28 points (1.9%) to 26,517.93; and the Russell 2000 rose 61.78 points (2.1%) to 2,979.77.

market_session: next scheduled US market holiday after Juneteenth
text: The next scheduled US equity-market closure after Juneteenth is Friday, July 3, 2026 (Independence Day observed); SIFMA recommends a 2:00 p.m. ET early bond-market close the prior day, Thursday, July 2, 2026.
